behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing; missing fields return one clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: A pure validator unit test can verify missing-field detection, but it can miss orchestration bugs where downstream steps still run despite validation failure. Testing the intake handler with downstream components wired and asserted-not-called catches the real boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the intake entrypoint with submissions missing multiple required fields. Assert: (1) error contains all missing field names in one response, and (2) insurance, triage, scheduling, and confirmation modules are not executed.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic date-parsing and range logic at a local function boundary with no I/O dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test DOB validator with boundary and invalid inputs: tomorrow, exactly 150 years ago, 150 years + 1 day ago, malformed strings, and valid dates. Assert expected accept/reject and error messages.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and correctly interprets eligible/not eligible/pending responses.
behaviors.justification: Contract correctness can fail in HTTP serialization, headers, or status/body handling that in-process mocks often hide. A real HTTP call boundary is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Execute eligibility check against an HTTP test server that validates request payload fields (patient ID, plan code) and returns realistic payer responses for eligible, not eligible, and pending. Assert correct internal status mapping for each response.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues instead of blocking.
behaviors.justification: Unreachability behavior depends on real network/subprocess error modes (timeout, connection refused, DNS issues) that mocks can underrepresent.
behaviors.test_description: Point payer endpoint to a non-routable host or closed port (and separately induce timeout). Run intake. Assert eligibility becomes pending, pipeline proceeds to triage/scheduling/confirmation, and no hard failure is returned.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model clinical reasoning/classification quality and can vary run-to-run. Mocked outputs cannot validate true triage quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage with a curated emergency-case set against the real AI triage model, repeat across multiple runs, and assert high-urgency assignment meets a minimum pass-rate threshold for emergency combinations.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage outputs a clinician-reviewable natural-language explanation, not only a numeric urgency score.
behaviors.justification: The explanation quality and presence depend on real model generation behavior; deterministic mocks can mask failures where the model returns empty, generic, or malformed text.
behaviors.test_description: Call real triage model for representative symptom inputs and assert response includes non-trivial natural-language rationale (not empty, not score-only), with clinician-readable sentence structure and symptom-to-urgency linkage checks.

behaviors.description: Appointment is scheduled in the first available slot matching urgency SLA: high=same day, medium=within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: This requires scheduling logic plus real calendar DB state interaction. Unit tests may miss query/order/time-window bugs across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with controlled availability. Run scheduling for high and medium urgency cases. Assert selected slot is earliest valid slot and meets same-day/48-hour constraints.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book a slot when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is transaction/locking behavior in shared DB under concurrent requests. In-process single-thread tests can miss race conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling operations targeting the same candidate slot against the real calendar DB with production-like transaction settings. Assert exactly one booking succeeds for that slot and the other is re-routed or rejected per policy.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Template rendering is simple, but correctness depends on wiring data from multiple modules (patient, scheduling, provider, visit-type rules). Unit-only tests can miss mapping omissions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute confirmation generation from a completed intake record with varied visit types. Assert message contains required fields and correct prep instructions for each visit type.

behaviors.description: Full intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end agent pipeline spanning deterministic and agentic stages; success depends on cross-step handoff plus real AI triage behavior within the sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end intake using real components: validation, real HTTP payer path (or realistic networked test endpoint), real AI triage call, calendar DB scheduling, and confirmation generation. Assert pipeline reaches completed state with persisted appointment and emitted confirmation without operator action.
